sources.name: Louisiana R.S. 23:341 — Pregnancy Disability Leave
sources.note: Primary statute. Employer threshold 25+ employees. Up to 4 months unpaid job-protected leave. Enacted 1983. [LAW]

sources.name: Louisiana R.S. 23:642 — Local preemption of paid leave mandates
sources.note: Act 97 of 1997. Bars parishes and municipalities from enacting paid leave ordinances. [LAW]

sources.name: Federal FMLA — DOL guidance
sources.note: 50-employee threshold, 12 weeks unpaid job-protected leave. Applies in Louisiana. [LAW]

sources.name: EEOC — Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA)
sources.note: Effective June 2023. 15+ employees. Reasonable accommodations for pregnancy. [LAW]

sources.name: Louisiana Commission on Human Rights — pregnancy leave guidance
Louisiana compliance cache (master-0106)
sources.note: UI wage base $7,000 (2026), 3% flat income tax, SUNA Solutions partner, at-will state. [TEAMED]

retrofit_needed.hero_note: Hero copied from Louisiana state-tax sibling (same Unsplash session, API rate-limited). Should replace with a distinct Louisiana photo in a fresh Unsplash session.
retrofit_needed.internal_links_note: Louisiana state parent and US country parent pages render as plain text per link gate (both wave-3/4). Auto-promote when targets ship.

cross_batch_sameness_anchors.opening_para_unique_fact: R.S. 23:341 applies at 25 employees — 25 fewer than FMLA threshold
